executiveSummary.text: AIG is a complex, restructuring multi-line insurer that has made meaningful progress simplifying its operations and strengthening its balance sheet, but the valuation at low-14x earnings is not compelling given persistent catastrophe volatility and execution uncertainty, warranting a REDUCE.

businessModel.text:
AIG is one of the largest insurance organisations in the world, operating through two core divisions following its multi-year restructuring:
    
      General Insurance (Commercial & Personal Lines)~65% of pre-tax income; commercial P&C, specialty lines, A&H, personal auto and homeowners
      Life & Retirement~35% of pre-tax income; individual life, group benefits, retirement accounts
    
    AIG's commercial insurance operations serve large multinational corporations, middle-market businesses, and specialty niche markets including political risk, cyber liability, and marine insurance. The personal lines business has been significantly scaled back following the 2021 Strawberry Hill reinsurance transaction. AIG earns revenues primarily through insurance premiums and investment income on the float generated by its insurance liabilities.

thesisEvaluation.text:
ScenarioScoreProbabilityCommentary
    
      Bull6630%Combined ratio improves to sub-95; catastrophe losses normalise; Life & Retirement delivers steady earnings; buybacks accelerate; EPS reaches $7.00-7.50; shares re-rate to 16-17x, reaching $100+.
    
    
      Base5750%Stable underwriting; modest Life & Retirement growth; CAT losses remain elevated but manageable; buybacks support EPS; shares drift to $80-85 on mid-teens P/E.
    
    
      Bear4020%Major CAT event (hurricane, earthquake) causes large losses; reserve deficiencies resurface; Life & Retirement interest margin compressed by rate environment; shares fall to $50-58.
    
  
  
    Conviction Score: 66x0.30 + 57x0.50 + 40x0.20 = 56.3
    The bear-case downside exceeds the bull-case upside at the current valuation, supporting a REDUCE rating.

bull.scenario: bull-case upside at the current valuation, supporting a REDUCE rating.

bear.scenario: bear-case downside exceeds the bull-case upside at the current valuation, supporting a REDUCE rating.

recommendation.text:
Rating: REDUCE
    AIG has made genuine progress simplifying its business and returning capital to shareholders, and the buyback programme is meaningful at current prices. However, the shares trade near the middle of their 52-week range with a P/E of 14x that leaves little margin of safety. A large catastrophe event or reserve charge could push the shares materially lower, and there are cleaner insurance franchise names available at comparable valuations. Reduce and await a better entry point.
    
      FrameworkDetail
      Entry point$76.87 (current); wait for pullback to $68-70 to initiate new positions
      Target price (12 months)$82-$87 on 14-15x base case EPS of $5.75-$6.00 (10-15% upside)
      Stop-loss$67.00 (below the 52-week low; would signal a fundamental deterioration in underwriting or balance sheet confidence)
      Position sizingReduce; avoid initiating new positions at current levels; preferred P&C alternatives offer better risk/reward
      Key trigger to upgradeSub-95 combined ratio achieved; clarity on CEO succession; reserve review completed with no deficiencies; pullback to $68-70

entryExit.text:
American International Group Inc (AIG) (AIG) — Entry / Exit Framework

Reduce / Trim: Above $78.41 — the stock is trading near or above the 52-week high. Capital should be rotated elsewhere.
Tighten or exit on any bounce above $84.56 — the risk/reward is poor at current levels.
Stop-loss (for remaining exposure): $89.21 — a close above the 52-week high would confirm the bull case is breaking down.
Target: $76.95 — partial mean reversion toward the middle of the 52-week range.
